title: Medical device and methods of use
abstract: Devices, systems, and methods are provided for breaking a kidney stone(s) into smaller particles, fragments, and/or stone dust; and removing the same from a patient. The medical device may include a tube having a distal end, a proximal end, a port located proximal of the distal end, and a length of the tube extending between the proximal end and the distal end. A first portion of the tube may be proximal of the port and have a first cross-sectional area, while a second portion of the tube may be distal of the port and have a second cross-sectional area smaller than the first cross- sectional area. A first lumen may extend from the proximal end to the distal end of the tube. A second lumen may in communication with the port to fluidly connect the proximal end of the tube with the port.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A medical device, comprising: a tube having a distal end, a proximal end, a port located proximal of the distal end, and a length of the tube extending between the proximal end and the distal end, wherein a first portion of the tube is proximal of the port and has a first cross- sectional area and a second portion of the tube is distal of the port and has a second cross-sectional area smaller than the first cross-sectional area; a first lumen extending from the proximal end to the distal end of the tube; and a second lumen in communication with the port and fluidly connecting the proximal end of the tube with the port. 2. The medical device of claim 1 , wherein the port is distal to the proximal end by a distance of approximately half to approximately three-quarters the length of the tube. 3. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: a laser fiber disposed within the first lumen. 4. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: a fluid supply assembly connected to the first lumen. 5. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first lumen is configured to simultaneously connect to the fluid supply apparatus and a laser control. 6. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein a laser extends from the laser control through the first lumen and to the distal end. 7. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: a vacuum source connected to the second lumen. 8. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the port is angled relative to a longitudinal axis of the second lumen. 9. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: an illumination device extending through the tube. 10. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: an imaging device extending through the tube. 1 1 . The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the first cross- sectional area is approximately 3 mm to approximately 8 mm. 12. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the second cross-sectional area is approximately 2 mm to approximately 5 mm. 13. The medical device of any of the preceding claims wherein the port is substantially crescent-shaped. 14. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the port is located approximately 5 cm to approximately 15 cm from the distal end. 15. The medical device of any of the preceding claims, wherein the second lumen terminates at the port.
